metric_definitions.research_accuracy: Token overlap between predicted and ground-truth industry labels.
metric_definitions.pain_point_accuracy: Fraction of GT pain points with sufficient token overlap in predictions.
metric_definitions.persona_accuracy: Role-family overlap between predicted buyer enum and GT persona text.
metric_definitions.email_quality: Weighted composite: company name (20%), pain overlap (40%), reference facts (25%), CTA (15%).
metric_definitions.reviewer_agreement: Agreement with expected reviewer decision derived from research + email quality.

notes: Official n=100 OutboundBench baseline after the seller-profile fix (2026-07-11): ICP and Persona agents now score/select relative to an explicit SellerProfile (config/seller_profile.json) instead of scoring in a vacuum. ICP accuracy dropped (91%->72%) as an expected, honest consequence: OutboundBench's icp_target ground truth is itself a generic, seller-agnostic heuristic, so a correctly seller-anchored score will diverge from it for companies outside that seller's specific target market. Persona accuracy improved slightly (27%->29%) after two rounds of prompt tuning to prevent the persona agent from collapsing to a single dominant answer (first VP Sales, then Head of AI) once seller context was introduced.
